reason: The text explicitly describes a conflict between Hebrew workers and Egyptian taskmasters (נֹגְשָׂיו - nogshav, oppressors) who represent Pharaoh's ruling administration. God declares: 'I have surely seen the affliction of my people which are in Egypt, and have heard their cry by reason of their taskmasters' (רָאֹה רָאִיתִי אֶת־ עֳנִי עַמִּי... מִפְּנֵי נֹגְשָׂיו). The passage describes Hebrew suffering, oppression (לַחַץ - lachatz), and crying out as a direct result of pressure from these taskmasters. Later, the text mentions God witnessing 'the oppression wherewith the Egyptians oppress them' (אֶת־ הַלַּחַץ אֲשֶׁר מִצְרַיִם לֹחֲצִים אֹתָם). This clearly establishes a conflict between Hebrew workers and agents of the Egyptian ruling elite.
